description: Title of the movie (drawn from the IMDb Top 1000 list).
How efficiently the film moves. 1 = dragged, 3 = deliberate / slow-burn, 5 = perfectly fast / thrilling pacing.
How fresh and unconventional the film's concept and execution feel. 1 = highly derivative, 5 = highly original.
Suitability for family/group viewing across ages. 1 = highly inappropriate for kids, 3 = safe but unengaging for kids, 5 = universally engaging for the whole family.
Overall quality of the plot and story construction.

range_or_values: Subset of: unpredictable, plot_twists, strong_pacing, tight_structure
description: Reasons the plot was rated favorably. Stored as a Python list literal (e.g. "['plot_twists', 'tight_structure']"); use ast.literal_eval() to parse. Empty list if none apply.

range_or_values: Subset of: plot_holes, predictable, cliche, narrative_inconsistency
description: Reasons the plot was rated unfavorably. Same list-literal format as above. Empty list if none apply.
Quality of dialogue writing. 1 = stilted / exposition-heavy, 5 = sharp and authentic.

range_or_values: happy, sad, disappointed, bittersweet, energized, neutral
description: The dominant emotion the ending evokes in the viewer.
Overall quality rating of the ending itself.
How the ending's construction/pacing felt structurally.
How absorbing the viewing experience is. 1 = disconnecting, 5 = hypnotic / fully immersive environment.
How memorable and resonant the film is afterward. 1 = instantly forgettable, 5 = leaves a massive lasting impression.
The model's holistic overall impression/enjoyment rating of the film.
Quality of cinematography and visual composition.
Quality and believability of visual/special effects (CGI and practical).
Whether the film has noticeable audio mixing issues (e.g. dialogue drowned out by music or effects).
Quality of the background score/musical score.
Quality of any featured songs or soundtrack music (as distinct from the background score).
Quality of the cast's acting performances.
How emotionally connected to the characters/story a viewer is likely to feel. 1 = detached, 5 = deeply invested.
Density of references, homages, or easter eggs to other works or culture. 1 = none, 5 = heavily referential.
Mental effort required to follow and understand the film. 1 = passive / easy to follow, 5 = demands close attention and active thought.
How much specialized prior knowledge (technical, historical, franchise lore, etc.) is needed to fully appreciate the film. 1 = none needed, 5 = substantial.
Level of flashing lights, strobing, or rapid visual changes that may affect photosensitive viewers. 1 = none, 5 = significant warning warranted.
Degree of depicted or implied harm to animals. 1 = none, 5 = significant / graphic.
How much the film's marketing/trailers reveal key plot points. 1 = trailer reveals little, 5 = trailer/marketing heavily spoils the plot.
